memberdef.argsstring: (const ClosedSetType type, const T &min_val, const T &max_val, const T &value)
memberdef.name: ClosedSet

memberdef.argsstring: (ClosedSetType type, const T &min_val, const T &max_val, const Node &node)
memberdef.name: ClosedSet

memberdef.argsstring: (const ClosedSetType type, const T &min_val, const T &max_val, const Nodes &antichain=Nodes())
memberdef.name: ClosedSet

briefdescription.para: Two closed sets are equivalent iff their type, borders and corresponding antichains are the same. They are not equivalent otherwise.
This function decides whether a set of elements is part of the closed set by subset-compraring the input with all elements of the antichain.

detaileddescription.para:
decides whether the closed set contains a given element
node
a given ordered vector of elements of the type T
true iff the given ordered vector belongs to the current closed set

briefdescription.para: This function decides whether a set of sets of elements is a part of the closed set by subset-compraring the input with all elements of the antichain.

detaileddescription.para:
decides whether the closed set contains a given set of sets of elements
nodes
a given ordered vector of ordered vectors of elements of the type T
true iff the given ordered vector of ordered vectors belongs to the current closed set

briefdescription.para: This function decides whether a given ordered vector of elements of the datatype T belongs to the discrete interval of the current closed set.

detaileddescription.para:
decides whether the given vector respects the borders
node
a given ordered vector of elements of the type T which will be inspected
true iff the given ordered vector respects the borders

briefdescription.para: Adds a new element to the closed set.

detaileddescription.para: If the element is already contained in the closed set, the closed set remains unchanged. Otherwise, the antichain will be recomputed.

inserts a new element to the closed set
node
a given node which will be added to the closed set

briefdescription.para: Performs an union over two closed sets with the same type and carrier.

detaileddescription.para: This function simply adds all the elements of the antichain of the first closed set to the second closed set

performs an union over closed sets
rhs
a given closed set which will be unioned with the current one
an union of the given closed sets

briefdescription.para: Performs an intersection over two closed sets with the same type and carrier.

detaileddescription.para:
performs an intersection over closed sets
rhs
a given closed set which will be intersectioned with the current one
an intersection of the given closed sets

briefdescription.para: Performs a complementation over a closed set.

detaileddescription.para: The result will contain nodes which are not elements of the former closed set. The complement of an upward-closed set is always downward-closed and vice versa.

performs a complementation over a closed set
a complement of a closed set

Contains discrete range borders, its type and the corresponding anti-chain. It is necessary to be able to evaluate relation operators <, <=, >, >=, ==, != over instances of this datatype T.
T
Datatype the closed set contains.
